props.caption: An uncrewed jet-powered wingman fired a live AIM-120 at a digital target over the Mojave — the first disclosed live shot from an autonomous combat aircraft, and a template for buying air-combat mass.

items.note: Federal agencies have rated the Spain–Argentina final on 19 July SEAR 1 and placed it inside a $625 million FEMA security-grant programme. The tournament’s stadiums have operated at 99.7% capacity.
An uncrewed jet-powered wingman fired a live air-to-air missile at a digital target. The first disclosed shot gives the US Air Force a live-fire marker for its planned mass-production programme.
A seventh night of US strikes coincided with a reported hit on a Kuwaiti power-generation and water-desalination plant. Hormuz traffic fell near a tenth of normal levels as Brent advanced toward $88.
The UN sanctioned six people and two entities tied to the AFC/M23 and FDLR. The designations identify organisations and commanders linked to eastern Congo’s minerals-and-militia economy.

The Bank of Korea delivered its first rate increase in about three and a half years, leaning on semiconductor-led growth. The AI-compute cycle has entered the central bank’s policy calculation through exports, currency pressure and domestic demand.

items.what: The UN Security Council’s Congo sanctions committee added six individuals and two entities on 17 July, including the Alliance Fleuve Congo and figures tied to the FDLR. The measures target political and military structures embedded in eastern Congo’s war economy.

Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt raised Germany’s security posture from an “abstract threat level” to a “high threat level,” saying attacks must be reckoned with at all times. Germany recorded 58,851 extremist crimes in 2025, up from 57,701 in 2024.

The United States allowed the national emergency behind Executive Order 13936 to expire and removed designations based solely on it. Hong Kong’s separate-status revocation and other sanctions remain, with Reuters reporting that 38 of 49 sanctioned individuals are still restricted.

A magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck 71 kilometres west-southwest of Puerto Madero on 17 July at a depth of 10 kilometres, shaking Guatemala and El Salvador. Authorities activated tsunami and coastal warnings before reporting no major damage.

Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-I lifted off from Sriharikota on 18 July, completing the first orbital launch by a private Indian company from Indian soil. The 22-metre rocket placed customer payloads into a 450-kilometre orbit on the Aagaman mission.

Hyundai Motor Group agreed to acquire SoftBank’s remaining stake in Boston Dynamics and take full ownership of the company. The deal consolidates a prominent humanoid-robot programme under a single automaker as funding and production competition intensify.
